question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 removes `segment-routing mpls` from its IS-IS IPv4 address-family.
R3 stops emitting SR sub-TLVs in its LSPs and stops processing remote
prefix-SIDs into its MPLS forwarding table. R3 is the BGP vRR for
service signaling and a core P-router — losing SR on R3 kills any
SR-TE policy whose transit traverses R3, even though plain IS-IS
forwarding and BGP still work.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R3, segment-routing mpls was removed from the IS-IS IPv4 address-family (and R3's Loopback0 prefix-sid removed).
Without segment-routing mpls under the IPv4 AF, R3 no longer advertises SR-Capabilities or Prefix-SID sub-TLVs in its LSPs and no longer installs remote prefix-SIDs into its MPLS forwarding table, so it falls back to plain IP forwarding; the IS-IS topology and adjacencies are unaffected. Since R3 is a core P-router (and BGP vRR), any SR-TE policy whose transit traverses R3 can no longer build its label stack and breaks, even though plain IS-IS forwarding and BGP still work.
Fix: restore segment-routing mpls under R3's IS-IS IPv4 AF and re-add the Loopback0 prefix-sid index 3.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; removing
SR-MPLS from the IS-IS AF is a quiet config event (IS-IS adjacencies
stay Up). Diagnostic ground truth lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R3 `show running-config router isis LAB address-family ipv4 unicast`: no `segment-routing mpls` line (baseline: present). Diff vs R4 / any peer — R3 is the only one missing it.
R3 `show running-config router isis LAB interface Loopback0`: no `prefix-sid index 3` line (was removed as a dependency before the segment-routing-mpls removal).
R3 `show segment-routing mpls connected-prefix-sid-map ipv4`: empty (baseline: R3's own 192.0.2.3/32 mapped to index 3; remote prefix-SIDs present).
Any router `show isis database verbose <R3-LSPID>`: R3's LSP no longer carries SR-Capabilities or Prefix-SID sub-TLVs.
R2 `show mpls forwarding`: label entries for prefixes whose IGP path traverses R3 disappear — plain IP forwarding remains, but SR label stacks collapse.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
 interface Loopback0
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   no prefix-sid index 3

configure terminal
router isis LAB
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  no segment-routing mpls

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  segment-routing mpls

configure terminal
router isis LAB
 interface Loopback0
  address-family ipv4 unicast
   prefix-sid index 3
